steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank comes after "an array of ______" and is followed by three semicolon-separated examples (the Sarulla plant; the Reykjanes plant; the McGinness Hills plant). The blank must introduce that list while keeping "in Indonesia" as a location modifier for the first example, matching the pattern "in Iceland" and "in the US" used in the other two.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'technologies, in Indonesia,': A comma after "technologies" joins two independent clauses with only a comma — a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — 'technologies: in Indonesia,': The colon after "technologies" correctly introduces the series of examples. "in Indonesia," then serves as a location modifier for the Sarulla plant, parallel to "in Iceland" and "in the US." ✓
  • Choice C — 'technologies in Indonesia:': Placing the colon after "Indonesia" restricts the colon to introducing the Sarulla example only and breaks the parallel structure with the other two examples. ✗
  • Choice D — 'technologies in Indonesia;': A semicolon here is used mid-sentence before any independent clause — misuse of the semicolon that also collapses "in Indonesia" into the noun phrase rather than treating it as a location modifier. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
